value.Title: Control System for Carbon Intensity Management in a Hydrogen Supply Network

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJP-M743-RSVX-H3T9-00000-00Control System for Carbon Intensity Management in a Hydrogen Supply Network2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:10:00ZLexisNexisControl System for Carbon Intensity Management in a Hydrogen Supply NetworkTargeted News ServiceAlexandria, Va.

ALEXANDRIA, Virginia, May 12 -- AIR PRODUCTS & CHEMICALS, INC., Allentown, Pennsylvania has been assigned a patent (No. US 12625475 B2, initially filed Aug. 18, 2023) developed by Pratik Misra, Breinigsville, Pennsylvania, and Sanjay Mehta, Orefield, Pennsylvania, for "Control system for carbon intensity management in a hydrogen supply network."

A computer-implemented method of providing hydrogen having a defined carbon intensity (CI) value to an end user location, the process comprising: selecting a total end-to-end maximum CI value for the hydrogen from production to delivery of the hydrogen to an end user location; receiving one or more feedstocks; receiving product CI values associated with each feedstock and/or the produced hydrogen; receiving demand data defining the end user demand for the hydrogen; receiving renewable power data; defining, in an optimization model, a plurality of constraints; generating, using the optimization model, a control strategy for control of the one or more industrial plants; and controlling the industrial plants in accordance with the values of the control variables to process the one or more feedstocks in order to provide a required quantity of hydrogen meeting the selected total end-to-end maximum CI value for use by an end user.

For more information about Targeted News Service federal patent awards please contact: Myron Struck, Editor, Direct: 703/866-4708, Cell: 703/304-1897, Myron@targetednews.com

value.Byline: By Beth Ann Miller Beaver County Times
value.Title: New entrance plaza opens at Pittsburgh Zoo & Aquarium

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJ1-J6R3-RTJ2-X027-00000-00New entrance plaza opens at Pittsburgh Zoo & Aquarium2026-05-09T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:10:00ZLexisNexisNEWSPg. A4New entrance plaza opens at Pittsburgh Zoo & AquariumByBeth Ann MillerBeaver County Times

A new entrance to the Pittsburgh Zoo & Aquarium opened on May 6, and guests planning a visit to the zoo should be aware of a temporary new policy intended to help speed up check-in time at the gate.

Visitors will see an entrance plaza with updated landscaping that includes native Pennsylvania plants, new restrooms and a wind tree sculpture "that generates renewable green energy as it collects both wind and sun," according to an April 21 announcement by the zoo.

Other improvements include enhanced security features, an access control system throughout the zoo, an emergency generator for the front gates, elevators and escalators and new perimeter fencing, according to the announcement.

Admissions will begin at the new entrance gate by the end of May, the announcement said.

The new entrance was the first project completed in a 20-year master plan to renovate the entire zoo, according to the announcement. Funding for this project was provided by the Henry L. Hillman Foundation, Allegheny County Regional Asset District, the Colcom Foundation and The Charity Randall Foundation.

Clear bag policy added

Along with the new entrance, visitors should know that the Pittsburgh Zoo & Aquarium has temporarily added a clear bag policy to help reduce the time spent checking in guests at the gate.

Other zoo policies remain the same, according to a statement The Times received on May 5 from the zoo.

"The Pittsburgh Zoo & Aquarium regularly evaluates and updates policies for the safety of its animals, staff and guests. As with current policy, visitors may continue to bring bags and coolers to the zoo, and we will continue to reserve the right to inspect them," the statement said.

"While clear bags are not required, they will speed the admission process. Prohibited items are listed on our website's Zoo Information page. We appreciate your support and look forward to seeing you on your next adventure!"

Here are the details on the clear bag policy, according to the zoo's website:

Clear plastic, vinyl or PVC bags that are no larger than 12" x 6" x 12" are permitted. This includes clear backpacks, tote bags and fanny packs that meet the size restrictions.

Standard one-gallon clear resealable freezer bags are permitted.

Small clutch purses, wallets and purses up to 4.5" x 6.5" in size are permitted, regardless of color.

Guests carrying items such as keys, phones, wallets, credit cards, combs, makeup, jackets and blankets can carry them in their pockets or separately.

Medical bags with medically necessary items and diaper bags are permitted after inspection at the gate.

value.Title: Scientists built a 'zero-gap' reactor that turns carbon dioxide into methane with 95% efficiency

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK2-6CG3-SHSR-G20T-00000-00Scientists built a 'zero-gap' reactor that turns carbon dioxide into methane with 95% efficiency2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:10:00ZLexisNexisSCIENCEScientists built a 'zero-gap' reactor that turns carbon dioxide into methane with 95% efficiencyTOI Science Desk

Scientists at Pennsylvania State University came up with an amazing 'zero-gap' microbial electrosynthesis reactor. This device converts carbon dioxide into methane with more than 95 per cent efficiency. A crucial challenge in renewable energy storage gets tackled here by merging water electrolysis and biological methanation into one powerful unit. To make this work well, the reactor uses a membrane-separated electrode setup that cuts down electrical resistance inside, allowing fast electron transfer and steady reactions.The methane produced is renewable and can replace fossil fuels as a carbon-neutral option; it fits perfectly into existing natural gas systems for easy transport and storage. This development is a big step forward toward creating a circular carbon economy. It turns a major greenhouse gas into a high-energy fuel that's easy to store and can be scaled up for industrial use with precision.Zero-gap reactor converts carbon dioxide to renewable methaneThe 'zero-gap' design changes the structure by placing a thin membrane between the electrodes. This setup shortens the path that ions travel. Research headed by Bruce Logan at Penn State shows that this arrangement cuts down internal resistance and energy loss, resulting in a Coulombic efficiency of over 95 per cent, as reported by Pennsylvania State University. In other words, almost all the electrical energy is efficiently used for chemical conversion instead of being wasted as heat or lost in side reactions.The process accelerated kinetics through localised gas productionTraditional methods often struggle with slow electron transfer to microbes; this reactor takes a different approach.

First, renewable electricity splits water into hydrogen gas. Nearby, methanogenic microorganisms at the cathode immediately use this hydrogen to reduce carbon dioxide into methane. Because these organisms are so close, the process speeds up by avoiding delays that come from diffusion.A net-zero solution for long-duration energy storage As noted in a report by Bioengineer, the reactor produces almost 7 litres of methane for every litre of its volume each day. This high level of output is crucial for making it practical in industry. Since the result is methane, it works perfectly with the current global natural gas pipelines and storage systems. This offers a net-zero method for storing energy over long periods without needing to create new distribution networks. For Reprint Rights: timescontent.com

value.Title: New recall petition filed against Huron County Commissioner Steve Vaughan
value.Overview: The Huron Daily Tribune Online (Bad Axe, Michigan), 520words

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK4-KMR3-RY62-D05P-00000-00New recall petition filed against Huron County Commissioner Steve Vaughan2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:08:35ZLexisNexisNew recall petition filed against Huron County Commissioner Steve VaughanKaty Snodgrass, Staff Writer

A new petition to recall Huron County Commissioner Steve Vaughan was filed Thursday afternoon after the county election committee deemed the original language too unclear for voters to understand.

Colfax Township resident Lily Dropiewski originally filed a petition earlier this month to recall Vaughan over his July 8, 2025, vote to approve Resolution 25-85. This authorized adding the 15,000-acre cap amendment for industrial-scale renewable energy projects to Zoning Ordinance 2024-01. However, the election committee rejected the petition on Tuesday, saying the language was not specific enough for voters to understand what commissioner was being targeted and why.

After the rejection, Dropiewski and her supporters spent a day redrafting the language, focusing on precision and clarity.

Robert Gaffke, who has supported Dropiewski throughout the petition process, said after the hearing Tuesday that this was not a setback but a learning lesson for them.

"This isn't something we do every day but we know what we have to do now," he said. "We're going to come back, file the recall again and hope and pray that it's adequate. And I think it will be this time."

Around 1:30 p.m. Thursday, Dropiewski filed a new petition with the following language:

"Recall of County Commissioner, Steve Vaughan of District 4 townships Oliver, Chandler, Meade and Colfax, voted to approve resolution 25-85 dated July 8, 2025. Which authorized to allow zoning ordinance 2024-01 section 15.02.11.5 project area to include 15,000 acres for use in utility scale solar energy facilities in county zoned townships."

Because Vaughan voted in favor of approving the 15,000-acre cap amendment, the petitioners believe his decision did not align with the interests of the township residents he represents. As a result, they believe he should be recalled from office.

Vaughan, however, argued during Tuesday’s meeting that he was being unfairly singled out even though a majority of commissioners also voted to approve the resolution.

"All seven members were present during that vote, and six of us voted yes. One commissioner voted no," Vaughan said. "So if I'm being singled out of six, it is discriminatory that the target is only myself."

With the new petition filed, a new clarity and factuality meeting will be held June 1 at 2 p.m. in the probate courtroom. At the meeting, the election committee — Probate Judge Julienne M. Ferris, County Clerk Lori Neal Wonsowicz and County Treasurer Sheryl L. Jahn — will review the petition’s language and determine whether it is factual and clear.

If the petition’s wording is approved, organizers of the recall effort would have to collect the signatures of 816 registered voters from District 4, which consists of Oliver, Chandler, Meade and Colfax townships.

If the signatures are certified, the recall measure would appear on the November 2026 ballot. The number of signatures required is based on the number of District 4 residents who voted in the November 2022 general election.

If the petition is rejected, the filer can appeal the decision in Huron County’s 52nd Circuit Court or refile new language.

value.Title: Franklin County advances data center rules at second public hearing, sets May 20 vote

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK3-R9X3-RS4X-J309-00000-00Franklin County advances data center rules at second public hearing, sets May 20 vote2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:10:00ZLexisNexisCOMMUNITYFranklin County advances data center rules at second public hearing, sets May 20 voteStaff Writer

The Franklin County Commissioners moved their proposed data center ordinance one step closer to a final vote at a packed public hearing in Chambersburg April 28, advancing a revised version of the regulations after public criticism prompted significant changes earlier in the month. A final vote on the ordinance has now been scheduled for May 20 at 6:30 p.m. The ordinance, written as an amendment to the county's existing Subdivision and Land Development Ordinance, or SALDO, is designed to set rules for the construction, installation and operation of data centers anywhere in Franklin County. County leaders have repeatedly emphasized that the ordinance is aimed less at attracting data centers than at making sure local communities have meaningful safeguards in place if a developer comes calling. Much of the urgency stems from a gap in local zoning law.

Pennsylvania's Municipalities Planning Code requires every municipality to allow legal land uses somewhere within its borders, meaning Franklin County cannot simply ban data center development outright. The SALDO amendment is designed to fill that gap for the county's eight non-zoned municipalities, which Commissioner Robert Ziobrowski has said represent roughly 20 percent of Franklin County's population. Without zoning ordinances of their own, those municipalities have no industrial districts where data center proposals could be directed, which makes the county's setback and performance standards especially consequential. That structural reality drew direct support from at least one local official. Lurgan Township Supervisor Brian Zimmerman thanked the commissioners during the hearing for offering a county framework that townships like his could rely on. "I appreciate the guidance that you're going to provide townships like ours without zoning," Zimmerman said. The most contentious revision to land in the updated ordinance was the removal of a requirement that data center developers generate renewable energy to offset at least 25 percent of a project's power use. Many residents had supported that requirement at the first public hearing on April 1, viewing it as a meaningful deterrent to large-scale development because of the substantial land commitment it would have required. The rule was cut after a consultant's analysis, presented to the board through Planning Director Quentin Clapper, concluded that a single data center built under the original requirement would have needed a solar farm of roughly 1,000 acres or more to satisfy the offset, raising concerns from county officials that the requirement could threaten farmland preservation more than the data centers themselves. Commissioner Chairman Dean Horst cited the same 1,000-acre figure in defending the change. The commissioners voted 2-1 at their April 8 meeting to accept the revision, with Ziobrowski casting the lone dissent. That change drew immediate pushback from residents at the April 28 hearing, several of whom argued that solar generation and agriculture are not mutually exclusive. "It is important to recognize that the land used for solar could easily be returned to agricultural use, or it can be co-oped and grazed simultaneously," resident Natasha Distasio told the commissioners. Distasio also urged the board to strengthen the ordinance's water protections, calling on the county to require data center operators to pay for on-site water discharge testing and to ensure any water leaving a facility matches the existing groundwater quality. "Require that all discharge be routinely tested," she said. "This also transfers the burden of the water treatment on-site to the company." Other speakers focused on the ordinance's landscape and buffer requirements, asking the commissioners to specify native plant species rather than simply banning invasive ones. Greencastle resident Jacob Reid told the board that he would like to see buffer requirements written with local ecology in mind. "To the requirement of buffer plants, I would love to see something, if it's possible, specifying more native species," Reid said. "Not just not invasive, but something that we know is local." The revised ordinance does retain a robust set of performance standards. Negative impacts such as noise, glare, vibration, smoke and odor must be imperceptible beyond a project's property line. The proposal also outlines a campus-style development model under which multiple data center facilities would be grouped into a single designated area rather than scattered across the landscape, and it includes specific provisions on noise control and water usage systems. George Pomeroy, chairman of the Franklin County Planning Commission, told the board the document does what a county-level ordinance should do. "It is my judgment and that of the planning commission that this is a good ordinance," Pomeroy said. Public comment has been heavy throughout the process. The county received approximately 100 comments at or before the April 1 hearing, including 79 written submissions, with all but one expressing opposition to data centers in Franklin County. After the planning department published the proposed revisions, the county received an additional 107 comments from the community. The commissioners have made clear that the SALDO amendment is a backstop, not the front line of any future data center fight. Commissioner John Flannery has urged residents to attend their local township and borough meetings, where decisions about specific data center proposals would ultimately be made. Both Flannery and Horst have said they have no knowledge of any company actively pursuing a data center site in Franklin County at this time. The commissioners' final vote on the proposed amendment is scheduled for 6:30 p.m. May 20 at the Franklin County Administration Building, 272 N. Second St., Chambersburg. The proposed ordinance is available for public review at franklincountypa.gov.

value.Title: The Biggest Winner in AI's Power Crisis (Not NVIDIA!)

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJK-VJ03-SFP5-520Y-00000-00The Biggest Winner in AI's Power Crisis (Not NVIDIA!)2026-05-11T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:10:00ZLexisNexisNEWSThe Biggest Winner in AI's Power Crisis (Not NVIDIA!)Keith Kohl

Of all the places on Earth that we would expect to embrace a next-gen technology to solve an energy crisis. Yes, the last area we'd look at is actually a quiet industrial estate near Redruth in Cornwall, England.

You see, for the first time in British history, geothermal electricity started flowing into the national grid.

I know the United Downs geothermal plant didn't look like much from the surface.

There are no visible cooling towers, nor are their smokestacks dotting the horizon.

All we see is just a modest facility sitting on top of the deepest onshore well ever drilled in the United Kingdom 5,275 meters straight down into granite.

For the record, that's over three miles deep.

At that depth, water hits 190℃.

That's hot enough to generate electricity using a binary Organic Rankine Cycle system and power approximately 10,000 homes. Twenty-four hours a day. Seven days a week. No wind required. No sunshine needed.

Yes, that's stable, baseload power feeding the grid 24 hours a day, with no wind or sun necessary.

Although the project took 15 years and £50 million to build, it proved something that had been theoretical for decades you don't need to live near a volcano to tap geothermal energy at scale.

You just need to drill deep enough.

More importantly, you need the right technology to do it.

My veteran readers know right away that I'm referring to Enhanced Geothermal (EGS). Think of it as geothermal energy liberated from geography… drill anywhere, then fracture hot rock and inject water, and you'll be ready to extract heat and generate power.

Now here's the kicker: the technology that makes it work came straight from the shale oil and gas boom that we've witnessed for more than a decade.

You see, the same techniques that unlocked American shale are now unlocking geothermal energy anywhere on the planet.

And while Cornwall was celebrating its breakthrough, they're not alone in wanting to tap into this clean underground baseload power. The U.S. DOE just said it's shelling out a $14 million grant for Pennsylvania's first Enhanced Geothermal Systems project in Indiana County, Pennsylvania.

As you might expect from a project in the Marcellus region, it's located at an existing natural gas well owned by CNX. They're converting a horizontal shale gas well into a geothermal well.

If this gambit is successful, it'll become a replicable model that could unlock geothermal potential across the entire eastern United States thousands of miles from the volcanic hotspots out West where conventional geothermal has been confined for decades.

Remember, it was less than three years ago that a DOE study estimated that enhanced geothermal could account for up to 12% of the nation's electricity by 2050.

Geothermal is finally getting the attention it deserves.

And now let me show you why…

The elephant in the room that nobody is talking about right now is the fact that AI data centers need 24/7 baseload power.

Unfortunately, that demand simply cannot be satisfied from intermittent power sources. They need to operate regardless of whether or not the sun is shining or the wind is blowing.

Constant, reliable, around-the-clock electricity.

But at this point, is this really a new revelation?

Not for us.

However, the power demand needed is starting to get all too real data center demand in the U.S. alone is projected to hit somewhere between 210 terawatt-hours and 1,540 terawatt-hours by 2030.

That's a massive range, but the direction is clear: Demand is exploding.

Again, solar and wind don't work for AI training running 24/7.

Of course, you'd also need batteries for multi-day coverage, and the cost at that scale is prohibitive.

Natural gas works (and will play a major role!), but with it comes the constant grips and pressure over the carbon emissions it makes and every major tech company has net-zero pledges.

Nuclear could work (and a strong option), but you're looking at 10-plus year timelines and regulatory battles that make drilling look easy by comparison.

But let's also be clear that Geothermal solves all of it.

Every one of those boxes are checked: 24/7 operations, steady baseload power, and it all comes carbon-free.

That's also not to mention the fact that the capacity factor is around 90%.

And make no mistake, Big Tech has taken notice… and they have that greedy glint in their eyes.

Recently, Google signed a 115-megawatt Enhanced Geothermal Systems deal with Fervo Energy for Nevada data centers, and then another 150 megawatts of conventional geothermal with Ormat Technologies.

Meanwhile, Meta inked a 150 megawatts deal with Sage Geosystems for a project east of the Rockies the first major EGS project announced outside the western U.S., as well as another 150 megawatts with XGS Energy in New Mexico.

Keep in mind that these aren't pilot projects. They're gigawatt-scale commitments backed by billion-dollar companies that need firm power and can't wait a decade for nuclear reactors to come online.

The interesting part that few understand is that EGS works because it's taking a playbook straight out of the shale boom.

You see, EGS uses the same horizontal drilling techniques developed for oil and gas. That's the same hydraulic fracturing that creates permeability in rock formations, and the same fiber-optic diagnostics to monitor downhole conditions in real time.

That's how Fervo Energy the company building the 500-megawatt Cape Station project in Utah has cut drilling times in half.

Early wells that took around a month to complete now take half that time.

In fact, the DOE's FORGE research site in Utah demonstrated drilling rates doubling from 8 meters per hour to 15 meters per hour, with peak rates hitting 26 meters per hour.

Today, Fervo's consistently hitting 30 meters per hour.

Those are shale-level drilling speeds applied to next-gen geothermal technology. And it's done using the same skill sets and supply chains as companies used during the tight oil and gas boom.

And that's why EGS is suddenly viable at scale.

Truth is, the oil and gas industry spent the last 15 years perfecting exactly the techniques geothermal needs to go from niche renewable to baseload workhorse.

Politically, geothermal energy is a win-win situation because EGS satisfies everyone.

The environmental crowd gets carbon-free, 24/7 power with no emissions. The all-of-the-above energy development crowd gets a technology built on American oil and gas innovation that creates jobs and delivers reliable baseload electricity.

You probably know just how rare bipartisan support for anything is these days.

Pennsylvania's $14 million grant to convert a shale gas well into geothermal is proof that everyone's on board.

Why? Because this isn't happening in California or Nevada. It's happening in the Appalachia region coal and gas country.

By 2050, the DOE puts geothermal potential at more than 90 gigawatts and this industry is still in its infancy.

Therein lies our opportunity.

The obvious plays that aren't much of a secret are the pure plays like Fervo Energy.

Right now, they're positioning for an IPO as they build the Cape Station project 500 megawatts by 2028, and all of it contracted to Southern California Edison, Shell Energy, Clean Power Alliance, and Google.

First power from the project should come online in the next six months.

So, don't be surprised that Fervo captures all the headlines.

After all, they ARE the growth story and first-mover in geothermal right now; the company is turning EGS from theory into commercial reality.

And of course… they'll IPO at a premium valuation because of it.

But here's what most people are missing: Fervo didn't invent the technology making EGS viable.

They're simply applying it.

So, what about the players hiding behind the curtain? You know, the ones that perfected horizontal drilling, hydraulic fracturing, fiber-optic monitoring, and high-temperature completion systems that got us to the point we are today?

Those are the stocks that'll take EGS to the next level.

And you can bet they're public, profitable, and still trading at valuations that would make Warren Buffett blush.

They're the ones supplying the rigs, the drill bits, the downhole tools, the completion systems, and the subsurface expertise that every EGS developer needs.

And still, the market's still pricing them as pure oil and gas service plays.

That's the gap we cannot ignore.

Perhaps it's time you take a quick minute and check out the full details behind this new technology unlocking geothermal forever.

Until next time,

Keith Kohl

Check us out on YouTube!

A true insider in the technology and energy markets, Keith's research has helped everyday investors capitalize from the rapid adoption of new technology trends and energy transitions. Keith connects with hundreds of thousands of readers as the Managing Editor of Energy & Capital, as well as the investment director of Angel Publishing's Energy Investor and Technology and Opportunity.

For nearly two decades, Keith has been providing in-depth coverage of the hottest investment trends before they go mainstream from the shale oil and gas boom in the United States to the red-hot EV revolution currently underway. Keith and his readers have banked hundreds of winning trades on the 5G rollout and on key advancements in robotics and AI technology.

Keith's keen trading acumen and investment research also extend all the way into the complex biotech sector, where he and his readers take advantage of the newest and most groundbreaking medical therapies being developed by nearly 1,000 biotech companies. value.Title: Conor Lamb and clean energy

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJ0-2Y43-SGCK-H19P-00000-00Conor Lamb and clean energy2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:10:00ZLexisNexisTribune-Review WestmorelandConor Lamb and clean energy

Regarding the op-ed by Kate Harper and Conor Lamb "Our energy future is 'Made in Pa.' " (April 14, TribLive): Lamb just can't help himself. He's rooted to the past and big industry, so proud that Pennsylvania is second in the nation for energy production. Not of clean, safe renewables, but gas and nuclear. You know, fracking, the industry that has amassed thousands of violations for polluting air and water, been implicated in studies for cancer, asthma, heart disease and other health problems, and destroyed thousands of acres of land for wells, pipelines and compressor stations.

China surges ahead, as do other countries, but here in the U.S., we have too many Conor Lambs. And there is no solution for radioactive nuclear waste -- still leaking in Hanford, Wash., Mr. Lamb.

According to the UN, for every dollar invested, renewable energy creates three times as many jobs as the fossil-fuel industry. Renewables accounted for 82% of all new energy jobs in 2024, far outpacing other energy sectors (Department of Energy). While fossil fuels are subsidized with millions of tax dollars and funds for renewables are being axed, utility bills rise because exported liquid gas escalates prices.

Pennsylvania has a wonderful opportunity to advance with energy that is cleaner and cheaper and protects the health of our families.

How about support for those sources, Mr. Lamb?

Jan Milburn

Ligonier

value.Title: Montauk Renewables swings to loss in first quarter as costs mount

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHJ-SWX3-SFKV-N24J-00000-00Montauk Renewables swings to loss in first quarter as costs mount2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:06:32ZLexisNexisCompany SignificantMontauk Renewables swings to loss in first quarter as costs mount

(Alliance News) - Montauk Renewables Inc on Thursday swung to a loss for the third quarter of 2026 as the company grappled with rising costs.

The Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania-based company focused on converting biogas into renewable energy suffered a pretax loss of USD1.3 million for the first quarter that ended March 31, flipped a profit of USD1.2 million a year earlier.

Total operating expenses rose 14% to USD48.0 million from USD42.2 million, mainly due to operating and maintaining renewable natural gas facilities.

Revenue for the first quarter was USD46.4 million from USD42.6 million, getting a boosts from renewable identification numbers sold from its GreenWave joint venture.

Montauk produced 1.4 million metric million British thermal units of renewable natural gas during the first quarter, flat compared to the same period a prior year. It produced aut 43,000 megawatt hours in renewable electricity in the first quarter, down 6.5% from 46,000 MWh.

Montauk said it had commissioned its Montauk Ag Renewables project in North Carolina and expects its production and revenue generation activities to commence this month.

"We expect a ramp-up in production volumes throughout 2026 directly related to additional feedstock collection," it said.

Shares in Montauk rose 0.7% to ZAR25.44 in Johannesburg on Thursday morning.

By Artwell Dlamini, Alliance News senior reporter South Africa

Comments and questions to newsroom@alliancenews.com

urn:contentItem:6JHM-GTK3-S197-Y18S-00000-00Montauk Renewables Inc (NASDAQ:MNTK) Posts First Quarter 2026 Results, Secures New Financing and Extends Gas Rights2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:06:31ZLexisNexisMontauk Renewables Inc (NASDAQ:MNTK) Posts First Quarter 2026 Results, Secures New Financing and Extends Gas Rights

AMERICAN DAILY STOCK REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

07 May 2026 05:43 EDT

PITTSBURGH, May 06, 2026 — Montauk Renewables, Inc. (NASDAQ:MNTK), a renewable energy company focused on biogas to RNG, posted its first quarter results for the three months ended March 31, 2026. In March 2026, Montauk signed a five-year senior credit facility with a wholly owned subsidiary of HASI that totals up to $200 million in senior indebtedness, with $45 million available to borrow under the agreement. Separately, Montauk secured a five-year gas rights extension at its Raeger facility through 2031 to ensure feedstock access. The EPA finalized 2026 and 2027 RFS standards and granted a partial waiver of the 2025 cellulosic biofuel volume requirement, establishing final 2026 and 2027 cellulosic RVOs at 1,360 million and 1,430 million D3 RINs, respectively. The company also commissioned the Montauk Ag Renewables project in North Carolina and expects production to commence in May 2026, with a ramp in 2026 as feedstock collection expands.

urn:contentItem:6JJX-8203-RTF0-P077-00000-00How GE Vernova May Navigate Contract Dispute Following ORIDEN, LLC v. GE PROLEC TRANSFORMERS, INC. Pennsylvania Breach Suit *Updated*2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:10:00ZLexisNexisHow GE Vernova May Navigate Contract Dispute Following ORIDEN, LLC v. GE PROLEC TRANSFORMERS, INC. Pennsylvania Breach Suit *Updated*

NYSE STOCK REPORT

What Stakeholders Need To Know

This proceeding illustrates how supply-agreement obligations within the power-transformer and energy-infrastructure industries can result in state-level breach allegations that transition to federal jurisdiction through removal. Organizations encounter prospective accountability when subsidiary or joint-venture operations are identified as defendants and corporate parent entities are noted in formal disclosures. The resolution of this matter may necessitate comprehensive industry evaluations of contractual stipulations, parent-liability frameworks, and risk-allocation mechanisms, potentially elevating compliance expenditures and altering procurement methodologies. Subsequent industry developments could encompass more stringent standards for vendor management and corporate reporting in transformer procurement for renewable energy and grid modernization initiatives, thereby influencing operational flexibility throughout the power-equipment sector.

Case Background

This litigation originates within the power-transformer industry, where GE Vernova conducts substantial business via its GE Prolec Transformers, Inc. subsidiary, providing equipment for utility, industrial, and data-center development. The plaintiff, ORIDEN, LLC, has initiated a breach-of-contract claim against the defendant, GE PROLEC TRANSFORMERS, INC. Initially filed in the Pennsylvania Court of Common Pleas, Allegheny County (GD-26-002212) on February 26, 2026, the dispute was subsequently transferred to the U.S. District Court for the Western District of Pennsylvania on April 2, 2026 (No. 2:26-cv-00554) pursuant to 28 U.S.C. § 1441. Concurrently, the defendant submitted a Disclosure Statement recognizing GE Vernova, Inc. as its corporate parent or affiliated entity. The action is classified under 190 Contract - Other Contract. The plaintiff operates as a renewable-energy developer backed by Mitsubishi, while the defendant is represented by James Patrick Cullen of Marshall Dennehey Warner Coleman & Goggin. Detailed contractual provisions, specific breach allegations, and requested damage figures currently remain sealed or undocumented in public judicial records.

Legal Proceedings

The litigation is currently in the initial post-removal stage before Judge David S. Cercone. The defendant submitted a Motion to Dismiss on April 9, 2026, with the briefing schedule expected to conclude in May 2026. A formal discovery timeline or trial commencement date has not yet been established. The proceedings remain active with customary preliminary filings, and standard industry sentiment indicates routine monitoring. The anticipated schedule suggests a judicial decision regarding the motion to dismiss within the upcoming months, which may precede subsequent discovery phases or settlement negotiations if the litigation advances.

Legal Outcomes and Policy Implications

Prospective resolutions include dismissal at the motion stage, negotiated settlement during the briefing period, or progression toward discovery and adjudication. A judgment favoring the plaintiff could underscore parent-company vulnerabilities in subsidiary contractual disputes, likely necessitating modifications to risk-allocation phrasing within transformer-supply agreements. Regulatory consequences may involve increased oversight by state commercial tribunals and federal diversity jurisdictions regarding corporate disclosures in energy-equipment commerce. This litigation corresponds with wider industry movements prioritizing ESG compliance in energy operations, wherein documented contractual adherence and transparent parent-entity participation are increasingly evaluated in governance frameworks and investor assessments.

Why This Case Is Challenging for GE Vernova

Legal Liabilities

Vulnerability to breach-of-contract allegations involving the GE Prolec Transformers, Inc. subsidiary, accompanied by potential parent-entity inclusion due to formal corporate affiliation disclosures.

Possibility of judicial determinations extending legal responsibility beyond the primary defendant within complex, multi-entity supply structures.

Financial Exposure

Anticipated legal defense expenditures and prospective settlement valuations reaching multiple millions, in addition to potential contractual damages or consequential financial impacts.

Requirement to maintain financial reserves for contingent liabilities, which may influence quarterly financial disclosures in a highly capital-intensive industry.

Reputational Damage

Allegations regarding contractual non-performance in the supply of transformers could influence stakeholder evaluations among utility providers and renewable energy developers.

Navigating public relations considerations within a sector that prioritizes dependable project fulfillment and supply-chain stability.

Operational Disruptions

Prospective requirements to modify contractual templates and parent-guarantee provisions across North American transformer distribution networks, potentially impacting procurement operations.

Temporary limitations on specific supply obligations while internal procedural assessments are conducted.

Regulatory Scrutiny

Intersection with federal removal guidelines and prospective examinations regarding adherence to commercial-contract disclosure regulations in the energy sector.

Elevated administrative requirements for documenting subsidiary-parent affiliations in official regulatory submissions.

Market Position

Increased monitoring by competitors regarding contractual dependability, which could introduce complexities in strategic bidding processes amidst current transformer-supply constraints.

Overall Impact on the Industry

This litigation could expedite the industry-wide implementation of definitive parent-liability disclaimers and standardized risk-allocation provisions among principal transformer manufacturers and service vendors. Competing firms and project developers may initiate concurrent audits of their respective supply contracts, thereby increasing expenditures on legal reviews and standardizing contractual protocols across grid and renewable infrastructure initiatives. Advancements in digital contract-management technologies might also increase as organizations attempt to minimize contractual breach vulnerabilities while adhering to strict project delivery schedules.

Navigating a Dual-Edged Sword

GE Vernova has the capacity to fortify its contract-governance frameworks and integrate transparent parent-disclosure standardizations to minimize prospective vulnerabilities while maintaining the necessary operational independence of its transformer subsidiaries. Proficient management of this matter could reinforce the organization's standing as a dependable energy-equipment supplier, potentially strengthening commercial relationships with renewable energy developers. Conversely, excessively restrictive policies could result in supplementary legal complications and commercial impediments within a highly competitive, project-centric supply environment. Calibrated modifications to governance structures present a method to control risks while sustaining market presence in power-transformer services.

Activities

GE Vernova, a spin-off from General Electric, is a global leader in the energy sector, focusing on innovative solutions for power generation, renewable energy, and electrification. The company's core activities encompass the design, manufacturing, and servicing of technologies for gas and steam power plants, wind turbines, hydroelectric systems, and grid management solutions, enabling reliable and efficient energy delivery worldwide. Its fastest-growing segments include offshore wind and electrification technologies, driven by rising demand for sustainable energy sources amid global decarbonization efforts. Additionally, GE Vernova leverages advanced digital platforms for predictive maintenance and asset optimization, enhancing operational efficiency for utilities and industrial clients. With a strong presence in markets like the U.S. NYSE, the company is positioned to capitalize on the transition to cleaner energy, supporting infrastructure development and energy security. It is the NYSE's largest Energy company by market capitalisation.

GE Vernova (NYSE:GEV) is poised as a leading force in the renewable energy sector, leveraging its expertise as a premier wind turbine manufacturer to drive sustainable progress globally. Central to its operations are the GE Vernova wind turbines, including advanced models like the GE Vernova Haliade-X 14MW and the GE Vernova Cypress onshore turbine. Strategic projects, such as the GE Vernova wind turbine supply Spain and the GE Vernova Spain wind project, underscore its robust market presence and capability in executing large-scale GE Vernova wind farm supply contracts. By focusing on innovative solutions like GE Vernova floating offshore turbines, GE Vernova grid connection solutions, and comprehensive GE Vernova O&M services, the company ensures superior turbine performance and efficiency. With a commitment to excellence, evidenced by the GE Vernova wind turbine maintenance and GE Vernova turbine commissioning, GE Vernova is strategically positioned for long-term growth, especially in green energy in Spain.

value.Title: States, Allies Seek New Mid-Atlantic Grid Policies To Boost Renewables

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJW-NT33-SD1X-G25D-00000-00States, Allies Seek New Mid-Atlantic Grid Policies To Boost Renewables2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:10:00ZLexisNexisStates, Allies Seek New Mid-Atlantic Grid Policies To Boost Renewables

May 12, 2026 Maryland Gov. Wes Moore (D) is pressuring the regional grid operator serving his state to take steps to promote renewables as the cheapest and quickest options for meeting rising energy demand, while ensuring that data centers pay for grid upgrades they require, as grid officials weigh steps to address soaring power demand forecasts.

At PJM Interconnection's May 11 annual meeting in Baltimore, Moore urged grid officials to quickly approve energy storage projects that would support intermittent renewable energy facilities. He also pushed for PJM's capacity auction in September to establish 15-year price certainty "to ensure the cost of new energy resources is allocated to those driving the need," according to a statement from his office.

While his state is adopting policies supporting such clean energy resources, Moore said "Maryland -- and no state alone -- cannot deliver meaningful, lasting relief unless we do it in partnership with PJM."

The push, echoed by environmentalists, comes as PJM considers improving its policies to alleviate a longstanding waitlist for connecting generation to the Mid-Atlantic grid, with environmentalists worrying some options being considered would favor natural gas and other fossil fuel generation.

The debate over PJM's interconnection queue has emerged as a microcosm of a broader national fight about how to meet the growing electricity demands of data centers and artificial intelligence.

The Trump administration favors expanded fossil fuel production and use, but environmentalists and state lawmakers outside the PJM meeting argued that approach would further drive up consumer costs.

Yet, Moore stressed that a growing number of states served by PJM are calling for an "all-of-the-above" approach to energy that includes both renewables and traditional sources such as coal and natural gas.

"So, when Pennsylvania and Maryland both can say that we are 'all-of-the-above' states, the truth is, we can use the same words -- but that still means a very different reality based on the makeup of our states," Moore said. "Maryland's reality is natural gas is a major and a necessary part of our energy mix today. We understand that and we embrace it."

But he continued that new gas plants are "not feasible for Maryland. Interconnecting just one new plant was estimated to cost $800 million."

"So, when I say 'all-of-the-above' for new generation, it means just that. But it means our priority is going to be investing in solar, and wind, and storage, and demand response. That's what that looks like in the state of Maryland.

And that can be implemented now -- not tomorrow, not next year -- to drive affordability for our people and to make sure that they can feel the impacts of action instead of just dealing with the consequences of inaction," Moore asserted.

Maryland Legislation

Moore spoke the day before he signed the Utility RELIEF Act, HB 1532, which includes an annual bidding process for renewable energy sources. The program will be implemented by the Maryland Public Service Commission and Maryland Energy Administration, leveraging $100 million from a Strategic Energy Investment Fund to support clean energy projects.

The bill also requires utilities to join PJM while forbidding them from passing on to consumers the cost of joining the grid operating entity.

"The mandate will promote accountability for utilities, saving Maryland families an estimated $20 million annually," according to a summary from Moore's office.

Also, the bill requires "data centers to pay for their own energy infrastructure upgrades and protects Maryland families. These guardrails aim to hold data centers accountable for responsible development -- neutralizing their impact on the grid and Marylanders' energy bills," the summary says.

Maryland state Sen. Katie Fry Hester (D), a co-sponsor of the legislation, said at a May 11 press conference outside the PJM meeting that the policies being urged for the regional grid are consistent with the White House's pledge that data centers pay for grid upgrades, even as she said the state is advancing a very different approach that would require data centers bring new clean energy to the grid.

"The demands [of PJM] we are making today are three: protect the public from increased reliability risks by ensuring the large loads operate flexibly until they are supported by deliverable new supply; remove the large loads that do not bring their new supply from the base-residual auction; and design the reliability backstop auction to include truly new resources," Hester said.

"Clean energy is champing at the bit to lower energy bills, but PJM is artificially slowing clean energy from coming onto the grid in order to benefit fossil fuel interests," said Maryland Del. Lorig Charkoudian (D) at the press conference. Charkoudian was a supporter of the Utility RELIEF Act in the House.

State lawmakers at the press conference said PJM policies are delaying the interconnection of lower-cost and cleaner energy projects, while "keeping more expensive power sources on the system -- contributing to higher electricity bills across the region," according to a statement by the Chesapeake Climate Action Network (CCAN).

Lawmakers cited an April 2025 report by Evergreen Collaborative that found PJM policies cost consumers about $500 annually. Environmentalists also cited cost savings from clean energy projects in other regions, such as the Vineyard Wind offshore project near Massachusetts that will save residents "$1.4 billion over the next 20 years," according to CCAN.

"PJM is failing us not by accident, but by design. It has been co-opted by fossil fuel energy generators and utilities and is quickly being overtaken by Big Tech and their data center companies as well. All the while, PJM's members have been raking in massive revenues due to energy market dysfunction. PJM won't help energy bills decrease until there is meaningful governance reform within PJM," said CCAN federal campaigns manager Jake Schwartz.

Other state lawmakers at the press conference included Pennsylvania Rep. Danielle Friel Otten (D) and New Jersey Assemblyman Dave Bailey (D).

Meanwhile, the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) on April 28 approved a PJM proposal to limit capacity auction wholesale electricity prices at $325 per megawatt-day and set a floor of $175 MW-day.

FERC's decision extends the price collar beyond the 2027-28 delivery years. "The extension of the price collar is part of a comprehensive set of reforms directed by the PJM Board of Managers to address the electricity supply-demand imbalance driven by rapid data center expansion," according to PJM.

The PJM proposal was supported by the White House Energy Dominance Council and the Energy Department, as well as about a dozen governors.

Moore attended a White House event in January with the governors of other PJM states to urge the regional transmission operator to encourage new energy sources including renewables, as well as to ensure data centers pay for grid upgrades.

The agreement "calls on PJM to accelerate the study of pending energy projects" and "to clear the backlog of generators waiting to connect to the grid. The principles aim to reduce interconnection timelines to 150 days or less, providing certainty for developers and speeding up the deployment of new energy resources," according to a Jan. 16 statement from Moore's office.

The 13 states served by PJM are Delaware, Illinois, Indiana, Kentucky, Maryland, Michigan, New Jersey, North Carolina, Ohio, Pennsylvania, Tennessee, Virginia, West Virginia, and the District of Columbia. -- Rick Weber (rweber@iwpnews.com)

value.Title: IKEA GROUP - INDUSTRY & PEER INSIGHTS: TRENDS AND HIGHLIGHTS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK4-CP13-RS50-G2SH-00000-00IKEA GROUP - INDUSTRY & PEER INSIGHTS: TRENDS AND HIGHLIGHTS2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:10:00ZLexisNexisSTOCKIKEA GROUP - INDUSTRY & PEER INSIGHTS: TRENDS AND HIGHLIGHTS

DASHBOARD: IKEA GROUP

PermID5001428642Websitehttp://www.ikea.com/us/en/IndustryFurniture & Home Goods RetailAddressLEIDEN ZUID-HOLLAND Netherlands

ACTIVITIES:

IKEA Group, founded in 1943 by Ingvar Kamprad, is a global home furnishings retailer headquartered in Delft, Netherlands. Employing over 225,000 people across 50+ countries, it operates 460+ stores, generating 45 billion annually. Known for affordable, flat-pack furniture and Scandinavian design, IKEA serves millions with products for home living. In 2024, it expanded e-commerce and urban micro-stores to meet digital shopping trends. Its culture emphasizes sustainability, inclusion, and employee growth, with initiatives like the IKEA Foundation supporting global communities. Competing with Wayfair, IKEA stands out for its circular economy focus, aiming for 100% renewable energy by 2030. Recent initiatives include second-hand furniture programs and AI-driven design tools.

+ IKEA GROUP [3 Press Releases]

+ IKEA GROUP SOLUPPGANG COLLECTION

IKEA introduces SOLUPPGANG - a new outdoor collection that encourages everyone to enjoy nature without a threshold. IKEA unveils SOLUPPGANG, a new outdoor collection designed to make everyday adventures simple, spontaneous and affordable for the many. Drawing inspiration from Nordic friluftsliv - the philosophy of open‑air living - and Japanese komorebi, the poetic fleeting beauty of sunlight filtering through leaves, SOLUPPGANG invites people to enjoy small, meaningful moments in nature -whether it's a park visit, a one-day trip to the beach or mountains, or enjoying outdoor cooking with friends and family.

Source: Company Website

+ IKEA GROUP: SOLUPPGANG COLLECTION

IKEA introduces SOLUPPGANG - a new outdoor collection that encourages everyone to enjoy nature without a threshold.

IKEA unveils SOLUPPGANG, a new outdoor collection designed to make everyday adventures simple, spontaneous and affordable for the many. Drawing inspiration from Nordic friluftsliv - the philosophy of open‑air living - and Japanese komorebi, the poetic fleeting beauty of sunlight filtering through leaves, SOLUPPGANG invites people to enjoy small, meaningful moments in nature -whether it's a park visit, a one-day trip to the beach or mountains, or enjoying outdoor cooking with friends and family.

Source: Company Website

+ IKEA GROUP EARTH MONTH NEWS FROM IKEA U.S.

Conshohocken, PA - April 1, 2026 - In celebration of Earth Month, IKEA U.S. is offering opportunities for customers to live more sustainably through a new food item, offers on circular services, and in‑store events.

"At IKEA U.S., we believe that taking collective and consistent actions can create meaningful change for our planet," said Mardi Ditze, Country Sustainability Manager at IKEA U.S. "Whether it's choosing a plant-based meal, repairing a piece of furniture using our free Spare Parts Service, or shopping second-chance items in the As-is section, we aim to make it easier for the many to take simple steps that add up to a better everyday life and a more sustainable future."

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ THE HOME DEPOT, INC. [1 Press Release]

+ NEW HOME DEPOT CREATOR PORTAL CONNECTS INFLUENCERS WITH HOME IMPROVEMENT ENTHUSIASTS TO DRIVE NEW REVENUE OPPORTUNITIES

ATLANTA, Dec. 10, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- The Home Depot today announced the launch of The Home Depot Creator portal, a creator-first platform designed to connect digital content creators to The Home Depot, helping them monetize their home improvement content and spark new ideas for future partnerships.

The Home Depot Creator portal is a centralized hub where creators can access content inspiration, campaigns opportunities and expertise to help them build content around home improvement, DIY projects and decor tips -- ultimately driving product recommendations and inspiration for homeowners, pros and DIYers everywhere. Not only do content creators connect with opportunities driven by The Home Depot brand, but also with its vast network of suppliers and products. Creators can earn commissions through shoppable links, access tools to maximize content opportunities and track performance, all through the portal.

Thousands of creators are already enrolled, and applications are now open to the public. Once applications are accepted, members will receive creator-focused onboarding and access to immediate content and campaign opportunities. Creators can utilize training resources, best practices, branded hashtags, curated collections and product catalogs to maximize their visibility with The Home Depot. In addition to training, participants benefit from storefront capabilities, special perks, product gifting and more.

"The Home Depot Creator portal isn't just a website - it's a community that drives meaningful engagement with current and future customers through talented personalities who give them confidence to tackle their home improvement projects," said Molly Battin, senior vice president and chief marketing officer at The Home Depot. "We know our customers love sports, so we're especially excited to launch this program with our Starting Lineup that will deliver tailored soccer content such as how to transform your backyard for your own neighborhood soccer matches and viewing parties."

The Home Depot Creator portal kicks off ahead of the largest soccer event in the world, with The Home Depot partnering with a roster of standout creators known as the Starting Lineup, to build excitement and passion for soccer's biggest stage.

The deep roster of designers, home improvement experts, athletes and Pros was built to ensure each creator's unique strengths and content style help provide support across a wide range of projects, with the help of The Home Depot, all while inspiring the next generation of homeowners.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ASHLEY FURNITURE INDUSTRIES INC. [3 Press Releases]

+ ASHLEY FURNITURE INDUSTRIES INC. FROM CLASSROOM TO CAREER: INSPIRING AREA STUDENTS WITH FACILITY VISITS & INDUSTRY INSIGHTS

ARCADIA, Wis. - Ashley Furniture Industries, LLC (Ashley) recently hosted a series of Career Day events to inspire students local to the company's Arcadia facility about their futures and provide real-world career exposure. Nearly 100 high school students from Arcadia, Blair-Taylor, Gale-Ettrick-Trempealeau, Independence and Whitehall explored career areas across the business.

Source: Company Website

+ ASHLEY INTRODUCES ASHLEY LUXE WITH HAILEE STEINFELD AS THE FACE OF THE BRANDS MODERN EVOLUTION

TAMPA, FL- Ashley, North America's largest furniture store brand, today announced the launch of Ashley Luxe, a new standard of modern elegance designed to make luxurious living beautifully attainable. Rooted in the belief that exceptional design shouldn't come with a premium price tag, Ashley Luxe brings together distinctive style, elevated materials, and refined craftsmanship. Academy Award-nominated actress, multi-platinum recording artist, and producer Hailee Steinfeld will serve as the face of the new line. Guided by her own modern aesthetic and love of home and design, Steinfeld naturally embodies the spirit of the collection - luxury made for everyday living.

Source: Company Website

+ ASHLEY OPENS NEW STORE IN CLERMONT, FL

CLERMONT, Fla. - On March 21, Ashley proudly unveiled its newest Ashley showroom with a Grand Opening and Ribbon-Cutting ceremony. The 48,000 sq. ft. facility, located at 17022 State Road 50, Clermont, FL 34711, marks a significant addition to the local community. Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES, INC. [2 Press Releases]

+ LOWE'S FOUNDATION PLANS EXPANDED $250 MILLION INVESTMENT TO HELP TRAIN AND DEVELOP 250,000 TRADESPEOPLE OVER NEXT DECADE, INCREASING SKILLED TRADES WORKFORCE COMMITMENT FIVE-FOLD

As the U.S. confronts a critical shortage of skilled trades workers, the Low e's Foundation today announced an expanded $250 million commitment to help train and develop 250,000 tradespeople by 2035 through its Gable Grants program. The announcement comes as Associated Builders and Contractors estimates that 349,000 net new construction workers are needed to meet demand this year alone.

The foundation's new goal builds on the early success following its 2023 announcement of a $50 million, five-year commitment to help train 50,000 tradespeople with the support of Lowe's vendor partners. With nearly $53 million invested in 65 organizations since then, the foundation is on track to help prepare 50,000 tradespeople by 2027, a full year ahead of its original goal, with momentum accelerating. "Three years ago, we set out to lead an ambitious workforce movement in a pivotal moment - and it's taken off faster than we imagined," said Marvin Ellison, Lowe's chairman and CEO and co-champion of the Business Roundtable "Skilled Trades for America" initiative. "American prosperity is at stake, and we are partnering to solve the workforce gap with a growing force of educators, employers and policymakers who understand local needs. No single organization can do this alone."

Source: Company Website

+ LOWE'S ROLLS OUT SPRING SAVINGS AND FREE SAME-DAY DELIVERY ON MULCH

Lowe's is kicking off peak home improvement season early as homeowners welcome spring by tackling outdoor projects and refreshing indoor spaces. As days grow longer and temperatures rise, demand for lawn and garden, patio and project essentials builds, making timing and value increasingly important for customers.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

Drawing inspiration from Nordic friluftsliv - the philosophy of open‑air living - and Japanese komorebi, the poetic fleeting beauty of sunlight filtering through leaves, SOLUPPGANG invites people to enjoy small, meaningful moments in nature -whether it's a park visit, a one-day trip to the beach or mountains, or enjoying outdoor cooking with friends and family.

Source: Company Website

+ IKEA GROUP: SOLUPPGANG COLLECTION

IKEA introduces SOLUPPGANG - a new outdoor collection that encourages everyone to enjoy nature without a threshold.

IKEA unveils SOLUPPGANG, a new outdoor collection designed to make everyday adventures simple, spontaneous and affordable for the many. Drawing inspiration from Nordic friluftsliv - the philosophy of open‑air living - and Japanese komorebi, the poetic fleeting beauty of sunlight filtering through leaves, SOLUPPGANG invites people to enjoy small, meaningful moments in nature -whether it's a park visit, a one-day trip to the beach or mountains, or enjoying outdoor cooking with friends and family.

Source: Company Website

+ IKEA GROUP EARTH MONTH NEWS FROM IKEA U.S.

Conshohocken, PA - April 1, 2026 - In celebration of Earth Month, IKEA U.S. is offering opportunities for customers to live more sustainably through a new food item, offers on circular services, and in‑store events.

"At IKEA U.S., we believe that taking collective and consistent actions can create meaningful change for our planet," said Mardi Ditze, Country Sustainability Manager at IKEA U.S. "Whether it's choosing a plant-based meal, repairing a piece of furniture using our free Spare Parts Service, or shopping second-chance items in the As-is section, we aim to make it easier for the many to take simple steps that add up to a better everyday life and a more sustainable future."

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ THE HOME DEPOT, INC. [1 Press Release]

+ NEW HOME DEPOT CREATOR PORTAL CONNECTS INFLUENCERS WITH HOME IMPROVEMENT ENTHUSIASTS TO DRIVE NEW REVENUE OPPORTUNITIES

ATLANTA, Dec. 10, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- The Home Depot today announced the launch of The Home Depot Creator portal, a creator-first platform designed to connect digital content creators to The Home Depot, helping them monetize their home improvement content and spark new ideas for future partnerships.

The Home Depot Creator portal is a centralized hub where creators can access content inspiration, campaigns opportunities and expertise to help them build content around home improvement, DIY projects and decor tips -- ultimately driving product recommendations and inspiration for homeowners, pros and DIYers everywhere. Not only do content creators connect with opportunities driven by The Home Depot brand, but also with its vast network of suppliers and products. Creators can earn commissions through shoppable links, access tools to maximize content opportunities and track performance, all through the portal.

Thousands of creators are already enrolled, and applications are now open to the public. Once applications are accepted, members will receive creator-focused onboarding and access to immediate content and campaign opportunities. Creators can utilize training resources, best practices, branded hashtags, curated collections and product catalogs to maximize their visibility with The Home Depot. In addition to training, participants benefit from storefront capabilities, special perks, product gifting and more.

"The Home Depot Creator portal isn't just a website - it's a community that drives meaningful engagement with current and future customers through talented personalities who give them confidence to tackle their home improvement projects," said Molly Battin, senior vice president and chief marketing officer at The Home Depot. "We know our customers love sports, so we're especially excited to launch this program with our Starting Lineup that will deliver tailored soccer content such as how to transform your backyard for your own neighborhood soccer matches and viewing parties."

The Home Depot Creator portal kicks off ahead of the largest soccer event in the world, with The Home Depot partnering with a roster of standout creators known as the Starting Lineup, to build excitement and passion for soccer's biggest stage.

The deep roster of designers, home improvement experts, athletes and Pros was built to ensure each creator's unique strengths and content style help provide support across a wide range of projects, with the help of The Home Depot, all while inspiring the next generation of homeowners.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ASHLEY FURNITURE INDUSTRIES INC. [3 Press Releases]

+ ASHLEY FURNITURE INDUSTRIES INC. FROM CLASSROOM TO CAREER: INSPIRING AREA STUDENTS WITH FACILITY VISITS & INDUSTRY INSIGHTS

ARCADIA, Wis. - Ashley Furniture Industries, LLC (Ashley) recently hosted a series of Career Day events to inspire students local to the company's Arcadia facility about their futures and provide real-world career exposure. Nearly 100 high school students from Arcadia, Blair-Taylor, Gale-Ettrick-Trempealeau, Independence and Whitehall explored career areas across the business.

Source: Company Website

+ ASHLEY INTRODUCES ASHLEY LUXE WITH HAILEE STEINFELD AS THE FACE OF THE BRANDS MODERN EVOLUTION

TAMPA, FL- Ashley, North America's largest furniture store brand, today announced the launch of Ashley Luxe, a new standard of modern elegance designed to make luxurious living beautifully attainable. Rooted in the belief that exceptional design shouldn't come with a premium price tag, Ashley Luxe brings together distinctive style, elevated materials, and refined craftsmanship. Academy Award-nominated actress, multi-platinum recording artist, and producer Hailee Steinfeld will serve as the face of the new line. Guided by her own modern aesthetic and love of home and design, Steinfeld naturally embodies the spirit of the collection - luxury made for everyday living.

Source: Company Website

+ ASHLEY OPENS NEW STORE IN CLERMONT, FL

CLERMONT, Fla. - On March 21, Ashley proudly unveiled its newest Ashley showroom with a Grand Opening and Ribbon-Cutting ceremony. The 48,000 sq. ft. facility, located at 17022 State Road 50, Clermont, FL 34711, marks a significant addition to the local community. Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES, INC. [2 Press Releases]

+ LOWE'S FOUNDATION PLANS EXPANDED $250 MILLION INVESTMENT TO HELP TRAIN AND DEVELOP 250,000 TRADESPEOPLE OVER NEXT DECADE, INCREASING SKILLED TRADES WORKFORCE COMMITMENT FIVE-FOLD

As the U.S. confronts a critical shortage of skilled trades workers, the Low e's Foundation today announced an expanded $250 million commitment to help train and develop 250,000 tradespeople by 2035 through its Gable Grants program. The announcement comes as Associated Builders and Contractors estimates that 349,000 net new construction workers are needed to meet demand this year alone.

The foundation's new goal builds on the early success following its 2023 announcement of a $50 million, five-year commitment to help train 50,000 tradespeople with the support of Lowe's vendor partners. With nearly $53 million invested in 65 organizations since then, the foundation is on track to help prepare 50,000 tradespeople by 2027, a full year ahead of its original goal, with momentum accelerating. "Three years ago, we set out to lead an ambitious workforce movement in a pivotal moment - and it's taken off faster than we imagined," said Marvin Ellison, Lowe's chairman and CEO and co-champion of the Business Roundtable "Skilled Trades for America" initiative. "American prosperity is at stake, and we are partnering to solve the workforce gap with a growing force of educators, employers and policymakers who understand local needs. No single organization can do this alone."

Source: Company Website

+ LOWE'S ROLLS OUT SPRING SAVINGS AND FREE SAME-DAY DELIVERY ON MULCH

Lowe's is kicking off peak home improvement season early as homeowners welcome spring by tackling outdoor projects and refreshing indoor spaces. As days grow longer and temperatures rise, demand for lawn and garden, patio and project essentials builds, making timing and value increasingly important for customers.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

In 2024, it expanded e-commerce and urban micro-stores to meet digital shopping trends. Its culture emphasizes sustainability, inclusion, and employee growth, with initiatives like the IKEA Foundation supporting global communities. Competing with Wayfair, IKEA stands out for its circular economy focus, aiming for 100% renewable energy by 2030. Recent initiatives include second-hand furniture programs and AI-driven design tools.

+ ASHLEY INTRODUCES ASHLEY LUXE WITH HAILEE STEINFELD AS THE FACE OF THE BRANDS MODERN EVOLUTION

TAMPA, FL- Ashley, North America's largest furniture store brand, today announced the launch of Ashley Luxe, a new standard of modern elegance designed to make luxurious living beautifully attainable. Rooted in the belief that exceptional design shouldn't come with a premium price tag, Ashley Luxe brings together distinctive style, elevated materials, and refined craftsmanship. Academy Award-nominated actress, multi-platinum recording artist, and producer Hailee Steinfeld will serve as the face of the new line. Guided by her own modern aesthetic and love of home and design, Steinfeld naturally embodies the spirit of the collection - luxury made for everyday living.

Source: Company Website

+ ASHLEY OPENS NEW STORE IN CLERMONT, FL

CLERMONT, Fla. - On March 21, Ashley proudly unveiled its newest Ashley showroom with a Grand Opening and Ribbon-Cutting ceremony. The 48,000 sq. ft. facility, located at 17022 State Road 50, Clermont, FL 34711, marks a significant addition to the local community. Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES, INC. [2 Press Releases]

+ LOWE'S FOUNDATION PLANS EXPANDED $250 MILLION INVESTMENT TO HELP TRAIN AND DEVELOP 250,000 TRADESPEOPLE OVER NEXT DECADE, INCREASING SKILLED TRADES WORKFORCE COMMITMENT FIVE-FOLD

As the U.S. confronts a critical shortage of skilled trades workers, the Low e's Foundation today announced an expanded $250 million commitment to help train and develop 250,000 tradespeople by 2035 through its Gable Grants program. The announcement comes as Associated Builders and Contractors estimates that 349,000 net new construction workers are needed to meet demand this year alone.

The foundation's new goal builds on the early success following its 2023 announcement of a $50 million, five-year commitment to help train 50,000 tradespeople with the support of Lowe's vendor partners. With nearly $53 million invested in 65 organizations since then, the foundation is on track to help prepare 50,000 tradespeople by 2027, a full year ahead of its original goal, with momentum accelerating. "Three years ago, we set out to lead an ambitious workforce movement in a pivotal moment - and it's taken off faster than we imagined," said Marvin Ellison, Lowe's chairman and CEO and co-champion of the Business Roundtable "Skilled Trades for America" initiative. "American prosperity is at stake, and we are partnering to solve the workforce gap with a growing force of educators, employers and policymakers who understand local needs. No single organization can do this alone."

Source: Company Website

+ LOWE'S ROLLS OUT SPRING SAVINGS AND FREE SAME-DAY DELIVERY ON MULCH

Lowe's is kicking off peak home improvement season early as homeowners welcome spring by tackling outdoor projects and refreshing indoor spaces. As days grow longer and temperatures rise, demand for lawn and garden, patio and project essentials builds, making timing and value increasingly important for customers.

Source: Company Website

DASHBOARD: IKEA GROUP

PermID5001428642Websitehttp://www.ikea.com/us/en/IndustryFurniture & Home Goods RetailAddressLEIDEN ZUID-HOLLAND Netherlands

ACTIVITIES:

IKEA Group, founded in 1943 by Ingvar Kamprad, is a global home furnishings retailer headquartered in Delft, Netherlands. Employing over 225,000 people across 50+ countries, it operates 460+ stores, generating 45 billion annually. Known for affordable, flat-pack furniture and Scandinavian design, IKEA serves millions with products for home living. In 2024, it expanded e-commerce and urban micro-stores to meet digital shopping trends. Its culture emphasizes sustainability, inclusion, and employee growth, with initiatives like the IKEA Foundation supporting global communities. Competing with Wayfair, IKEA stands out for its circular economy focus, aiming for 100% renewable energy by 2030. Recent initiatives include second-hand furniture programs and AI-driven design tools.

+ IKEA GROUP [3 Press Releases]

+ IKEA GROUP SOLUPPGANG COLLECTION

IKEA introduces SOLUPPGANG - a new outdoor collection that encourages everyone to enjoy nature without a threshold. IKEA unveils SOLUPPGANG, a new outdoor collection designed to make everyday adventures simple, spontaneous and affordable for the many. Drawing inspiration from Nordic friluftsliv - the philosophy of open‑air living - and Japanese komorebi, the poetic fleeting beauty of sunlight filtering through leaves, SOLUPPGANG invites people to enjoy small, meaningful moments in nature -whether it's a park visit, a one-day trip to the beach or mountains, or enjoying outdoor cooking with friends and family.

Source: Company Website

+ IKEA GROUP: SOLUPPGANG COLLECTION

Source: Company Website

+ IKEA GROUP EARTH MONTH NEWS FROM IKEA U.S.

Conshohocken, PA - April 1, 2026 - In celebration of Earth Month, IKEA U.S. is offering opportunities for customers to live more sustainably through a new food item, offers on circular services, and in‑store events.

"At IKEA U.S., we believe that taking collective and consistent actions can create meaningful change for our planet," said Mardi Ditze, Country Sustainability Manager at IKEA U.S. "Whether it's choosing a plant-based meal, repairing a piece of furniture using our free Spare Parts Service, or shopping second-chance items in the As-is section, we aim to make it easier for the many to take simple steps that add up to a better everyday life and a more sustainable future."

Source: Company Website

+ THE HOME DEPOT, INC. [1 Press Release]

+ NEW HOME DEPOT CREATOR PORTAL CONNECTS INFLUENCERS WITH HOME IMPROVEMENT ENTHUSIASTS TO DRIVE NEW REVENUE OPPORTUNITIES

ATLANTA, Dec. 10, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- The Home Depot today announced the launch of The Home Depot Creator portal, a creator-first platform designed to connect digital content creators to The Home Depot, helping them monetize their home improvement content and spark new ideas for future partnerships.

The Home Depot Creator portal is a centralized hub where creators can access content inspiration, campaigns opportunities and expertise to help them build content around home improvement, DIY projects and decor tips -- ultimately driving product recommendations and inspiration for homeowners, pros and DIYers everywhere. Not only do content creators connect with opportunities driven by The Home Depot brand, but also with its vast network of suppliers and products. Creators can earn commissions through shoppable links, access tools to maximize content opportunities and track performance, all through the portal.

Thousands of creators are already enrolled, and applications are now open to the public. Once applications are accepted, members will receive creator-focused onboarding and access to immediate content and campaign opportunities. Creators can utilize training resources, best practices, branded hashtags, curated collections and product catalogs to maximize their visibility with The Home Depot. In addition to training, participants benefit from storefront capabilities, special perks, product gifting and more.

"The Home Depot Creator portal isn't just a website - it's a community that drives meaningful engagement with current and future customers through talented personalities who give them confidence to tackle their home improvement projects," said Molly Battin, senior vice president and chief marketing officer at The Home Depot. "We know our customers love sports, so we're especially excited to launch this program with our Starting Lineup that will deliver tailored soccer content such as how to transform your backyard for your own neighborhood soccer matches and viewing parties."

The Home Depot Creator portal kicks off ahead of the largest soccer event in the world, with The Home Depot partnering with a roster of standout creators known as the Starting Lineup, to build excitement and passion for soccer's biggest stage.

The deep roster of designers, home improvement experts, athletes and Pros was built to ensure each creator's unique strengths and content style help provide support across a wide range of projects, with the help of The Home Depot, all while inspiring the next generation of homeowners.

Source: Company Website
